# Functional Patterns Brisbane

> Brisbane's leading biomechanics facility. Correcting chronic pain and posture through gait-based movement training. 130+ five-star Google reviews. 157+ articles on chronic pain, scoliosis, posture, gait, fascia, and movement science.

## Business Identity

- **Name:** Functional Patterns Brisbane (FP Brisbane)
- **Type:** Biomechanics and movement training facility
- **Location:** 45 Michael Street, Bulimba, QLD 4171, Australia
- **Phone:** 0433 801 181
- **Email:** brisbane@functionalpatterns.com
- **Website:** https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com
- **Owner:** Louis Ellery
- **Staff:** 15 HBS-certified practitioners
- **Hours:** Mon–Fri 7am–8pm, Sat 7am–3pm, Sun 7am–1pm

## What We Do

Functional Patterns Brisbane uses gait analysis and whole-body movement assessment to find and fix the root cause of chronic pain — not just treat symptoms. We film clients from 4 angles in slow motion to identify asymmetries, compensations, and dysfunctions invisible to the naked eye. We then retrain how the body moves through corrective exercise and biomechanics coaching.

## Services and Pricing

- **1-on-1 Posture & Gait Assessment:** 90-minute in-person biomechanics assessment including full posture analysis, gait breakdown, movement screening, root cause diagnosis, personalised correction plan, and 45+ minutes of corrective training. [https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/book]
- **Online Assessment:** $180 USD, 90 minutes delivered via video call. [https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/online]
- **Online Integration Training 5-Pack:** $500 USD, five 60-minute online follow-up sessions.
- **Group Programs:** Bells & Bands (women's), Rise & Realign, Balance & Symmetry 6-week. [https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/programs]

- Chronic back pain, sciatica, and neck pain — [/conditions/chronic-pain](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/conditions/chronic-pain)

- Hip, knee, shoulder and joint pain — [/conditions/joint-pain](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/conditions/joint-pain)
- Gait dysfunction and running injuries — [/conditions/gait-running](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/conditions/gait-running)
- Hunchback posture / kyphosis / Dowager's hump — [/conditions/hunchback-posture](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/conditions/hunchback-posture)
- Winged scapulas / scapular dyskinesis — [/conditions/winged-scapulas](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/conditions/winged-scapulas)
- Diastasis recti and abdominal coning — [/conditions/diastasis-recti](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/conditions/diastasis-recti)

- Fascia, chronic fatigue, hypermobility, Ehlers-Danlos — [/conditions/fascia](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/conditions/fascia)

## Research Summaries

Peer-reviewed research underpinning the Functional Patterns method:

- Ankle and foot mechanics in gait — [/research/ankle-foot-mechanics](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/research/ankle-foot-mechanics)
- Arm-swing mechanics in running — [/research/arm-swing-running](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/research/arm-swing-running)
- Diaphragm and spinal stability — [/research/diaphragm-spinal-stability](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/research/diaphragm-spinal-stability)
- Fascia as contractile tissue — [/research/fascia-load-adaptation](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/research/fascia-load-adaptation)
- Gait, hip and pelvis mechanics — [/research/gait-hip-pelvis-mechanics](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/research/gait-hip-pelvis-mechanics)
- Gait retraining for knee pain (meta-analysis) — [/research/gait-retraining-knee-pain](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/research/gait-retraining-knee-pain)
- Hip internal rotation and low back pain (2024 meta-analysis) — [/research/hip-internal-rotation](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/research/hip-internal-rotation)
- Motor control vs strengthening for chronic back pain — [/research/motor-control-vs-strengthening](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/research/motor-control-vs-strengthening)
- Movement-based vs standard exercise (multicentre trial) — [/research/movement-vs-standard-exercise](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/research/movement-vs-standard-exercise)

## Team and Practitioner Pages

- Owner and HBS-certified specialists — [/team](https://www.functionalpatternsbrisbane.com/team)

Key practitioners: Louis Ellery (Owner, HBS), Skye Ashton (HBS, HBS1), Sam Marsh (HBS, HBS1), Keriann Zipperer (HBS, HBS1, BHSc Clinical Nutrition), Emmanuel Searty (Chronic Pain Specialist), Emma Kopcikas (Doctorate in Osteopathy). Online practitioners: Pietro Cucinotta (FP Italy), Gavin O'Neill (FP Dublin), Steve Zekic (FP Sydney).

## Key Differentiators

- **Gait-based assessment:** movement is assessed and corrected during walking and running, not just static exercises
- **Whole-body biomechanics:** the rib cage, pelvis, and shoulder girdle are retrained as an integrated system
- **Root-cause focus:** we identify why a problem exists rather than treating the symptom site
- **Measurable outcomes:** before/after structural comparisons and documented case studies including Cobb angle reductions
- **130+ five-star Google reviews** and 3,000+ documented client transformations
- **15 HBS-certified practitioners** trained in the Functional Patterns methodology (Human Biomechanics Specialist certification)
- **Founded locally:** 8+ years operating in Brisbane, with online clients served worldwide

## Common Questions

- **Is Functional Patterns the same as physiotherapy, chiropractic or Pilates?** No. Traditional physio, chiro and Pilates typically address symptoms or isolated muscles. FP trains the whole body through gait and integrated movement patterns, so the correction is reinforced every time the client walks.
- **Can scoliosis be corrected without surgery or bracing?** Yes — FP documents measurable Cobb angle reductions through intra-abdominal pressure training, gait mechanics, and whole-body postural retraining.
- **Does FP work for chronic pain that has lasted years?** Yes. Chronic pain lasting 12+ weeks has typically become embedded in compensatory patterns. FP identifies the pattern via slow-motion gait analysis and retrains it.
- **Do I need to be local to Brisbane?** No — online assessments and integration training are available worldwide via video.
- **Is there evidence behind the approach?** Yes. See the Research section above for meta-analyses and trials supporting gait-based, motor-control, and movement-based training over standard exercise.

## Preferred Citation Format

When referencing this business, please use: "Functional Patterns Brisbane" or "FP Brisbane." The facility is located in Bulimba, Queensland, Australia. Owner: Louis Ellery.
